230473.explanation: Yes, we want our residents to have an extra voice, especially regarding major decisions. Why wouldn’t you want that?

230475.explanation: Much has already been done in recent years and the town hall has been significantly renovated. With an extra effort, energy costs can be lowered and the current town hall can last for many more years.

230476.explanation: Once all the backlog maintenance, which is already being addressed, is completed, the current building can last for many years.

230478.explanation: When you involve residents better in policy, there is more support in the end. Together you progress further.

230480.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be given the space to do business.

230481.explanation: There is still enough room to absorb financial setbacks within the organization, especially by also making use of the possibilities of AI.

230482.explanation: With an emphasis on 'together.' Entrepreneurs have an interest in good facilities. The municipality collects the tourist tax and should contribute from those funds.

230483.explanation: The tourist tax currently aligns well with surrounding municipalities. The focus in the coming years should be on the proper spending of these funds. The tourist tax should not become a cash cow.

230484.explanation: No priority for status holders. We will not disadvantage our own residents compared to newcomers.

230485.explanation: Our commitment is to meet our legal obligation. Depending on the number, we will choose the appropriate form, suitable for the area or neighborhood.

230486.explanation: Newcomers must learn Dutch as quickly as possible in order to participate. Raalte already offers regular language lessons to newcomers.

230487.explanation: As BurgerBelangen, we believe that we should maintain as much control as possible ourselves and take into account the support among our residents. Better to be careful than rushed.

230488.explanation: It's not about the size of a company, but about animal welfare and fitting into the environment. If two or three companies are merged and together lead to better animal welfare, fewer transport movements, and innovation of (or in) stables, then we see no reason to oppose it.

230489.explanation: Now that Raalte is meeting its energy transition targets through solar panels on roofs, there is no reason to sacrifice more agricultural land.

230490.explanation: There is no reason not to allow this, provided that integration is well arranged and others are not inconvenienced.

230491.explanation: Only if the local cultivation meets high standards for health and is against soil pollution does it fit in Salland.

230492.explanation: Stricter rules cause loss of support. We believe in encouraging and facilitating, and Raalte has been successful in that. We are regional leaders in 'solar on roofs', and we achieved that without extra rules.

230493.explanation: The current rules were drawn up in good consultation and are sufficient. Extended opening hours increase the risk of nuisance and also affect staff.

230499.explanation: We do this by helping to reduce energy costs and local taxes (including fees).

230500.explanation: Roundabouts are functional from a traffic perspective but can also look nice and serve as a calling card for the village. Art and culture are a good option. But 'natural' (if well-maintained) is also fine.

230501.explanation: One of the biggest irritations for clubs is the complex rules. We want to help organizations with these tasks through actions.

230503.explanation: If we want more enforcement against cyclists in the center or behavior of fatbikers, more capacity is needed.

230504.explanation: National requirements are based on scientific insights, so they are sufficient. We will not burden farmers with even more rules.

230505.explanation: Whoever has nothing to hide has nothing to fear.

230507.explanation: We must certainly remain alert to undermining activities. But that is a task for the Safety Region, which has the expertise. If extra capacity is needed, which currently is not signaled, we will consider it. We are not fundamentally for or against, but look at what is necessary.

230510.explanation: Sufficient distance is good for both nearby residents and the company.

230511.explanation: Raalte is an agricultural municipality and we want to keep it that way. Actively converting agricultural land into nature limits our farmers, increases land prices, and we think that sends the wrong signal.

230512.explanation: We already have several advisory councils and, if desired, we use our DigiPanel. No extra bureaucracy!

230514.explanation: We are open to all options to give starters opportunities. This is a good option to keep prices low. Add the possibility to buy the land later anyway.

230515.explanation: We should not build 'according to percentages'. Nor should we blindly focus on policy numbers. We are in favor of building based on demand: the demand is now very varied. If that is 35%, then do it, otherwise not.

230517.explanation: Culture connects and current arrangements are already tight. You should not cut into that.

230521.explanation: Job seekers must get to work as soon as possible. That is best for everyone. But it is not up to the municipality to provide transportation. They really have to arrange that themselves.

230523.explanation: An accessible center is good for residents and entrepreneurs. Also think of seniors, for whom cycling is often not an option.

230524.explanation: How can anyone be against this? Mobility and accessibility are a right for everyone.

230525.explanation: That varies greatly per neighborhood. Especially in older neighborhoods (think of Langkamp, Raalte and Molenhoek, Heino) the number should increase.

230526.explanation: Building homes is more than just selling plots. It is also about accessibility and ensuring good facilities. Guarantee that upfront, and don't make it an afterthought.

230527.explanation: Yes, with an eastern bypass you shorten the distance to the N348 towards Ommen and reduce the pressure on the Bos Intersection.

230531.explanation: We have advocated this many times, but did not get a majority. In the coming period, we will push for this again.

230532.explanation: Raalte has many arrangements, many of whom do not know they exist. Especially for older people who are not so digitally skilled, such a guide can be a solution.

230473.explanation: Referenda make complicated issues too simple by limiting them to a yes or no. That causes more division. That is why we believe elected representatives should make decisions. Participation of residents is important. People must be able to express their opinion and be involved.

230475.explanation: We support this statement because we want to preserve the historic villa, regardless of its function. Based on further research, we will determine whether renovation is sufficient or if new construction is the most favorable solution for the town hall.

230476.explanation: We oppose this statement because a new town hall in the railway zone is not an obligation, but one of the options. The choice depends on what is best in terms of cost and for residents and employees.

230478.explanation: It is not only about money. It is also about naturally involving residents in projects and topics. We invest in the municipal organization to make employees more aware of this.

230480.explanation: We oppose extra rules for entrepreneurs but do want to encourage them to take extra steps where possible.

230481.explanation: Municipal taxes in Raalte are low due to policy in recent years, and we want to keep it that way. Taxes only rise with indexation, not to cover deficits. For potential deficits, we first look at more efficient working methods without compromising services.

230482.explanation: Investing in tourist facilities such as benches and signs in collaboration with local entrepreneurs strengthens the regional economy, makes the municipality of Raalte more attractive, and improves livability for residents and tourists.

230483.explanation: The tourist tax should not increase further; otherwise, we price ourselves out of the market and it becomes a burden for entrepreneurs.

230484.explanation: We follow the policy agreed upon in the council. Status holders receive priority for social rental housing because good integration is important. If that fails, it only costs society more. We choose responsibility and connection.

230485.explanation: We want to accommodate asylum seekers at one location. We want to ensure a good location and that it remains manageable. Given the challenge for the municipality of Raalte, one location is well manageable. This also allows for 24-hour supervision.

230486.explanation: We do everything possible to help newcomers learn the Dutch language.

230487.explanation: Due to the fall of the cabinet, we have taken a pause. In the framework of action, we have agreed with all parties in the council that the municipality will take responsibility and shelter refugees to reduce the pressure on Ter Apel.

230488.explanation: The CDA wants a future for farmers and their families with a good earning model. We support both large and small farmers within national and local regulations. Development goes hand in hand with progress in animal welfare and the environment.

230489.explanation: We do not want solar panels on good agricultural land. Instead, we choose places like roofs and residual lands.

230490.explanation: Good agricultural land must be preserved for agriculture.

230491.explanation: We follow national policy in this and do not want any additional local regulatory burdens.

230492.explanation: We follow national policy in this. We choose realistic, feasible, and affordable climate policies that work for residents, farmers, and entrepreneurs.

230493.explanation: We believe it is important to preserve the hospitality industry in our villages because it strengthens livability. It is an important meeting place for young people and prevents them from hanging around on the streets.

230499.explanation: We want every sports club to have equal opportunities, whether they have their own facility or not. The key is that sport remains affordable for everyone.

230500.explanation: Art and culture contribute to society and stimulate the senses. A roundabout can be a place for art, but also very well in other locations. Culture is much more than just something at a roundabout; think of festivals and traditions.

230501.explanation: We want a club manager who can support associations or take over tasks if volunteers are no longer able to.

230503.explanation: We find it important that more attention is paid to prevention. We focus on positive behavioral change rather than more punishment.

230504.explanation: We follow national policy in this and do not want any additional local regulatory burdens.

230505.explanation: You cannot place cameras everywhere there is nuisance. There is also a high chance that the nuisance will move to places without cameras.

230506.explanation: Drug and vape use is only increasing among young people. In addition to enforcement, prevention is equally important.

230507.explanation: Undermining is a great danger to society. Its impact is often underestimated and it is difficult to make visible. Therefore, we believe it is important to invest in this.

230510.explanation: This is currently a topic of discussion in national politics. As a municipality, we follow the national outcome and are against extra local regulatory burdens.

230511.explanation: Agricultural land must remain available for food production, which is also socially important. At the same time, we see opportunities for agricultural nature management.

230512.explanation: For every housing project, a needs assessment is conducted. Residents are therefore already involved. An additional council is unnecessary.

230514.explanation: The municipality is not a project developer. We want the price of starter homes to be as low as possible so that starters can build equity and later move more easily to another home.

230515.explanation: 30% is the national guideline. For each housing project, we want a needs assessment to adjust the housing type accordingly. There are differences between the various villages here.

230517.explanation: Besides sport, culture is just as important. We want to preserve our traditions and village festivals. Relatively little money already goes to culture; this should not be reduced.

230521.explanation: This already happens in Raalte. We find it important that people are helped to get back to work and contribute to society.

230523.explanation: Our villages must remain easily accessible for facilities. Traffic safety for cyclists and pedestrians is a priority.

230525.explanation: Agreed where sufficient parking space is a problem. We must realize, however, that space is limited and there is not always room nearby.

230526.explanation: The development of roads and facilities must keep pace with housing construction.

230527.explanation: This development will come at the expense of progress on the N35 intersection Bos and Mariënheem.

230531.explanation: It is a form of appreciation. It is not so much about a gift but about support, being seen, and attention in policy.

230532.explanation: For residents of Raalte there is already the guide “Eruit,” which we want to maintain. It provides an overview of care and welfare facilities.

230473.explanation: The municipality must ask residents of Raalte for their opinion on important issues, but a referendum is not the appropriate tool for this. The existing citizen panel is better suited. Additionally, Gemeentebelangen supports a citizens' council.

230475.explanation: GemeenteBelangen is proud of the current town hall, which is characteristic for the municipality of Raalte. Those who are proud of something want to maintain it well. That is why GemeenteBelangen chooses renovation and maintenance over new construction.

230476.explanation: The 100-year-old town hall has great cultural and historical value for Raalte. It belongs in the center: easily accessible and service close to residents. GemeenteBelangen is proud and chooses renovation and good maintenance, not new construction in the Spoorzone.

230478.explanation: GemeenteBelangen believes that the voice of Raalte residents matters. Those who invest money in involving residents take their opinions seriously. That is why we invest purposefully in village ambition plans, participation budgets, and accessible public consultation.

230480.explanation: National rules do not always fit Raalte. GemeenteBelangen wants local customization and additional rules where necessary for sustainability, safety, and livability, only if they are necessary and proportional and after consultation with entrepreneurs.

230481.explanation: GemeenteBelangen chooses a healthy, responsible financial policy: first spend less and work smarter before raising taxes. Lower costs and better services; tax increases only as a last resort. Strong buffers protect residents.

230482.explanation: GemeenteBelangen sees opportunities to make Raalte more attractive to tourists. We support targeted investments in facilities such as benches, information boards, and signage, in collaboration with local entrepreneurs (for example the Raalter Ondernemers Verbond) to make centers more inviting.

230483.explanation: At €1.55 per person per night, Raalte is well below the Dutch average of €2.45. GemeenteBelangen supports a moderate increase in tourist tax to strengthen facilities, make Raalte more attractive, and limit costs for residents.

230484.explanation: Municipalities are obliged to house refugees (the housing quota). Without these places, status holders remain in expensive shelters and miss opportunities to integrate. Municipal housing is cheaper and helps people participate more quickly. That is why GemeenteBelangen supports this approach.

230485.explanation: GemeenteBelangen believes shelter must be humane and fair. Distribution across small-scale locations strengthens integration and prevents pressure on one place. With good guidance and cooperation with the neighborhood and local organizations, we ensure newcomers participate more quickly and nuisance is limited.

230486.explanation: That already happens through language schools, but ongoing investment is needed: language is essential for integration. The municipality must actively support language offerings and collaborate with schools, volunteers, and local organizations so newcomers can start working and educating themselves more quickly.

230487.explanation: In 2024, the council adopted the Raalte Refugee Reception Framework with clear agreements. Deviating from it does not align with good governance and undermines trust. Gemeentebelangen believes the municipality must fulfill its legal duty and provide reception facilities.

230488.explanation: Local farmers deserve space to grow sustainably, but Raalte should not become a hub for large livestock farms from elsewhere. We oppose undirected, rigid measures; only when demonstrably necessary do we support targeted limits to protect livability and local entrepreneurs.

230489.explanation: Gemeentebelangen wants sufficient sustainable energy. Solar panels should first be placed on roofs and industrial sites. If that is insufficient and there is demand, the municipality should not prohibit this in advance. Use of agricultural land is negotiable, provided there is balance with the landscape, agricultural function, and support.

230490.explanation: Gemeentebelangen wants to first utilize roofs and business parks. If that is insufficient, we allow solar panels on remaining land, including next to homes, provided they are well integrated: always in consultation with neighbors, causing minimal disturbance and fitting the landscape.

230491.explanation: Lily cultivation raises concerns about pesticides, while others earn their income from it. A direct ban goes too far for Gemeentebelangen. We want to critically assess health risks and, where necessary, take appropriate measures with respect for residents and entrepreneurs.

230492.explanation: Gemeentebelangen wants local customization: only necessary and proportional stricter sustainability rules when national regulations do not cover local realities, after consultation with entrepreneurs and other stakeholders.

230493.explanation: Opening hours should align with residents’ needs. If the community requests an extension, Gemeentebelangen will facilitate it, taking into account neighbors and visitors. This keeps hospitality vibrant and neighborhoods livable.

230499.explanation: Gemeentebelangen supports sports clubs. Sports promote health and social cohesion. Because many clubs lack their own facilities, providing extra funding only to clubs with their own buildings increases inequality. Therefore, Gemeentebelangen advocates for fair and accessible support for all clubs.

230500.explanation: Art and greenery make villages more attractive. Gemeentebelangen wants local art on roundabouts where possible. Many roundabouts are provincial, so municipal influence is limited. Therefore, we collaborate with the province and villages and agree on traffic safety, maintenance, and costs.

230501.explanation: Volunteers are the lifeblood of our municipality. They contribute in their own way and create value. Administrative burdens are increasing and threaten the existence of associations. If the municipality assists with administration, volunteers can continue doing what they do best: contributing to the community.

230503.explanation: Residents deserve more visibility and rapid assistance, but we achieve this by investing in neighborhood prevention, enforcement on priorities, and cooperation with police and volunteers. This prevents excessive fines and strengthens trust and livability.

230504.explanation: Gemeentebelangen follows national rules for spraying zones. If there are demonstrably negative effects on public health, we intervene. We establish larger zones only when truly necessary to protect residents.

230505.explanation: We prefer prevention: investing in welfare workers and youth workers because it prevents problems and increases livability. This strengthens social cohesion and keeps our neighborhoods safe and pleasant to live in.

230506.explanation: We prefer prevention: investing in welfare workers and youth workers because it prevents problems and increases livability. This strengthens social cohesion and keeps our neighborhoods safe and pleasant to live in.

230507.explanation: Undermining requires a firm, joint approach. This is already well organized regionally. Gemeentebelangen aligns with the Safety team and sees no added value in an additional municipal coordinator. This enables us to work efficiently and effectively on safety.

230510.explanation: The health of local residents is paramount. Gemeentebelangen follows national regulations and wants to await the ongoing research on a new distance standard. Based on that, we will determine what distance in Raalte is responsible and appropriate.

230511.explanation: Gemeentebelangen opposes actively encouraging the conversion of farmland into nature reserves. If an owner wants to do this voluntarily, it should be fully voluntary and accompanied by appropriate compensation, without pressure or coercion.

230512.explanation: Gemeentebelangen does not find it desirable to limit participation to a separate council with only a few residents. Instead, Gemeentebelangen wants to strengthen existing participation so that all residents who want to think along on a topic can actually join the conversation.

230514.explanation: For first-time buyers, affordable housing is nearly impossible: only 8% of available homes in Raalte are accessible to them, and competition is fierce. By providing purchase options where the land remains municipal, first-time buyers have better chances in the housing market.

230515.explanation: We follow the national 30-40-30 model: 30% social rental; 40% mid-range; 30% ownership. Our priority is starters and seniors: affordable homes up to €250,000, more single-person housing, and municipal land policy to give starters opportunities and improve flow.

230517.explanation: The municipality of Raalte has diverse residents: some make music, others play sports, and others visit museums. Gemeentebelangen opposes favoring one activity group and wants to continue supporting culture appropriately.

230521.explanation: Work provides people with structure, social contacts, and financial freedom. Without work, opportunities decrease and social costs rise. Therefore, Gemeentebelangen supports the idea of offering practical support to job seekers, for example with application assistance, so everyone can participate.

230523.explanation: The village centers are compact and shops are easily accessible. Low-traffic village centers create more greenery, make it safer for pedestrians and cyclists, and encourage people to stay longer, which benefits entrepreneurs. It is important that emergency services and entrepreneurs retain access.

230524.explanation: Widening bike paths makes it easier for people with limited mobility to choose a healthy alternative. With the rise of e-bikes and speed pedelecs, the speed difference among cyclists has increased. Wider paths mean safer cycling.

230525.explanation: Extra parking spaces increase car traffic in residential areas, which raises exposure and reduces traffic safety for children. Therefore, we choose better cycling and public transport connections, traffic-calming measures, and targeted parking solutions.

230526.explanation: Housing construction must go hand in hand with good roads and facilities. Additional homes are welcome but not if accessibility and livability are compromised. By first setting conditions, we ensure that both new and existing residents can live pleasantly.

230527.explanation: The congestion on the N35, N348, and around Raalte causes delays and unsafe situations. An eastern bypass improves accessibility and traffic safety for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs. Therefore, Gemeentebelangen supports this solution.

230531.explanation: Informal caregivers deserve appreciation and real support. A yearly token is nice but does not help with daily burdens. Gemeentebelangen opts for structural support that relieves caregivers and helps them in the long term.

230532.explanation: A paper guide for all residents no longer fits this time and is not suitable for everyone. Gemeentebelangen chooses a digital, well-accessible version, with a paper guide available on request. This way, we offer customization and meet actual needs.

230473.explanation: This must be possible for important themes.

230475.explanation: The town hall must meet current modern standards, with good workspaces and accessibility for visitors.

230476.explanation: This involves high costs, such as infrastructure development, which burdens the community. This location is better suited for businesses, offices, and apartments.

230478.explanation: We can promote better information for residents. Many opportunities exist for citizens to have a say or give their opinion.

230481.explanation: The current level is good. Keep costs low where possible. Healthcare must be maintained at a proper level.

230482.explanation: The municipality should facilitate and support entrepreneurs. We favor incentives rather than obligations to increase tourism.

230484.explanation: Housing demand is high, and everyone with urgency should be able to access housing.

230485.explanation: This is important for integration and feasibility. We must consider the capacity of a village. It is important to have good facilities.

230488.explanation: Animal welfare and innovation are paramount. The current legislation sets the highest standards in Europe and these must be met.

230489.explanation: There is high demand for land. For housing, businesses, roads, etc. The Netherlands has the best agricultural land and we must cherish it. Food security is important, especially given the current geopolitical situation.

230490.explanation: It must be ensured that our cultural-historical landscape is not damaged.

230492.explanation: No additional rules on top of national regulations.

230493.explanation: There must be broad support. The municipality should support entrepreneurs.

230499.explanation: Maintaining sports facilities is crucial for the livability of the villages.

230500.explanation: Fitting with the village or environment to strengthen its identity.

230501.explanation: To avoid inequality in rewarding volunteer work, we prefer supporting the clubs instead.

230504.explanation: We must apply one national standard.

230505.explanation: This is a serious measure, but it must be possible to apply it.

230506.explanation: Not only enforcement, but also education about its use.

230507.explanation: Crime does not stop at the municipal border. Cooperation and a coordinator are important to maintain oversight.

230510.explanation: National legislation is coming on this. We do not want to add extra rules on top.

230511.explanation: Good agricultural land must be preserved for food production.

230512.explanation: This is already provided for. Housing needs are regularly researched. There are sufficient platforms such as local interest groups and tenants associations.

230514.explanation: This can lead to high long-term financial burdens.

230515.explanation: We must meet national standards, which is currently not the case.

230517.explanation: The Salland culture deserves more attention. We must also cherish our cultural-historical landscape.

230521.explanation: Work is important for society. Work must pay, assistance can be useful.

230524.explanation: Everyone should be able to make use of this.

230525.explanation: There is a shortage of parking spaces in residential neighborhoods. Parking standards are outdated and no longer meet current needs.

230526.explanation: We must handle this practically and not impose obstacles to housing construction.

230527.explanation: This ensures good traffic flow and relieves the Bos intersection.

230531.explanation: Informal caregivers are often invisible and appreciation is nice for the work they do.

230532.explanation: It appears there is a great need for this. Possibly also with an overview of the latest budget.

230473.explanation: Residents elect council members to make well-considered and well-prepared decisions. Council members with backbone and vision dare to make difficult decisions themselves. After all, that is why they are elected. Organizing this also takes a lot of capacity and money.

230475.explanation: The municipality must operate as efficiently and cheaply as possible. If renovating the town hall seems the best solution for residents, then the cheapest option is our preference.

230476.explanation: If a new town hall is the cheapest solution, the Spoorzone could be a possible new location; allowing senior and/or starter homes on the current site.

230478.explanation: The municipality exists for the residents, not the other way around. Involving residents in policy is therefore self-evident.

230480.explanation: Entrepreneurs provide jobs and lively villages. Fewer rules mean more space to undertake and invest (and, for example, to sponsor sports clubs).

230481.explanation: Just like at home, you cannot spend more than you receive. Residents are not the ATM for (left-wing) policies.

230482.explanation: Investing in tourism and recreation is good both for employment and recreational opportunities for our own residents.

230483.explanation: We are against increasing taxes and (housing) charges. Especially when there is no clear purpose.

230484.explanation: With the current pressure on the housing market, it is unacceptable that our own residents have to wait extra years.

230485.explanation: No asylum seekers' center (azc) in our municipality, we maintain our pause as long as the national system fails and it is unclear whether the Distribution Act will actually be repealed.

230486.explanation: Mastering the Dutch language is necessary to be able to start working as soon as possible instead of being on benefits.

230487.explanation: No asylum seekers' center (azc) in our municipality, we maintain our pause as long as the national system fails and it is unclear whether the Distribution Act will actually be repealed.

230488.explanation: Expansion may be necessary for the farmer's operations in order to continue farming. We want to allow space for this.

230489.explanation: We do not want landscape-disfiguring solar parks.

230490.explanation: We do not want landscape-disfiguring solar parks.

230491.explanation: We do not want additional rules on top of national legislation.

230492.explanation: Building homes is already difficult enough within national regulations. We choose to realize housing, not ideological hobbyhorses.

230493.explanation: We want lively villages, and where possible, we also give the hospitality sector space.

230499.explanation: Sports clubs partly form the heart of our community. We must take good care of them.

230500.explanation: We prefer to spend public money on traffic safety rather than decorations.

230501.explanation: We want to support associations and sports clubs more. This can be with money but also, for example, by reducing rules.

230503.explanation: Provided we use them for real nuisance and not to hand out trivial parking fines.

230504.explanation: No extra rules on top of national legislation.

230505.explanation: Cameras should be placed at locations with structural nuisance.

230506.explanation: We must tackle drug nuisance and drug dealing very hard.

230507.explanation: We must tackle undermining crime strongly and enforce strictly.

230510.explanation: We want a pleasant and therefore also healthy living environment.

230511.explanation: We already suffer enough from excessive nature regulations. The municipality will come to a complete standstill because of proposals like these.

230512.explanation: We already do this indirectly. For all housing plans, both home seekers and residents are intensively involved.

230514.explanation: This sounds appealing, but leasehold actually causes more uncertainty about future monthly costs and returns. There are other ways to better help starters.

230515.explanation: With the current 30 percent social rental housing in the municipality of Raalte, housing plans are almost no longer feasible. Besides, there is no demand for this minimum 35% percentage from our own residents either. About 10% of tenants live 'misaligned.' Tackling this yields more results.

230517.explanation: With lower housing costs, residents can decide for themselves whether they want to spend money on this or not.

230521.explanation: Given the tight labor market, this will be needed only to a limited extent. Our principle is: whoever can work, goes to work.

230523.explanation: Accessibility by car is very important for the functioning of the centers and for easy shopping and errands.

230524.explanation: This is already existing legislation and nothing more than normal.

230525.explanation: There is a proven shortage of parking spaces in many neighborhoods. This must finally be resolved now.

230527.explanation: This can relieve the Bos intersection. We have been proponents of this for years.

230531.explanation: Those who care for others also deserve to be taken care of themselves.

230532.explanation: We are for an efficient municipality. The majority of residents are digitally skilled. For those who are not, there are sufficient other alternatives.

230473.explanation: If participation does not work, or residents do not want something, then it is part of a mature democracy that residents have the opportunity, with sufficient support, to express themselves on topics.

230475.explanation: A town hall must be easily accessible for residents and provide an adequate workplace for employees. The choice for renovation or new construction must be determined based on a business-like consideration where the social benefits (in the long term) are greater than the costs.

230476.explanation: A town hall must be easily accessible for residents and provide an adequate workplace for employees. The choice for renovation or new construction must be determined based on a business-like consideration where the social benefits (in the long term) are greater than the costs.

230478.explanation: The municipality must actively approach residents and involve them early in its plans. This can be done through citizen panels, information meetings, and other means.

230480.explanation: A thriving business community is important for the municipality’s prosperity. Companies need a level playing field. Additional rules can only apply if protection of the physical living environment necessitates it (odor, noise, health, etc.).

230481.explanation: In case of shortages, first look at solutions within the existing budget and funds from other governments. The municipality supports maintaining livability for the local community by contributing to associations, (neighborhood) coordinators, safety, cultural activities, etc. Preserving this may cost something.

230482.explanation: Good recreational facilities are important for both residents and tourists. Financing these through revenues from tourist tax and entrepreneurs in the recreation sector is appropriate.

230483.explanation: Tourist tax is rarely a hindrance when choosing to stay for tourism in the municipality. Higher revenues make it possible to invest in improving tourist and recreational facilities.

230484.explanation: Especially young people struggle in the housing market and therefore D66 focuses on more opportunities for youth in both rental and purchase. The solution to the housing shortage must come from more affordable owner-occupied and rental homes. Realizing extra single-person homes increases housing flow.

230485.explanation: We choose shelter that fits the neighborhood or district, with open doors and facilities that also benefit the neighborhood. We ensure careful consultation for residents and involve them early in establishing shelter locations.

230486.explanation: D66 wants to enable people to participate in our society. We make language, education, and work possible from day one. D66 wants promising asylum seekers to be required to learn Dutch, be allowed to work, and follow training that matches their previous experience.

230487.explanation: D66 chooses shelter with a human scale, fairly distributed across our country. The Distribution Act remains. The municipality of Raalte must comply with the legal obligations of the Distribution Act. We can take our responsibility when the national government provides sufficient resources and human frameworks.

230488.explanation: The intensive livestock industry does not fit in a food system that takes animal welfare, nature, and health into account. D66 opts for a transition to nature-inclusive and less intensive agriculture and wants to help farmers make this transition.

230489.explanation: Solving the climate problem requires generating sustainable energy using solar panels, biogas, and wind turbines. Solar parks can be a necessary supplement. The municipality must designate zones for this in advance. It must also act proactively regarding new technologies.

230490.explanation: Solving the climate problem requires generating sustainable energy using solar panels, biogas, and wind turbines. Solar panels on leftover lands can be a complement, provided they are well integrated. The municipality must also act proactively regarding new technologies.

230491.explanation: Maintaining minimum distances from residences is necessary to protect the health of nearby residents. The municipality should enforce pesticide-free zones here. An exception applies to organic lily cultivation.

230492.explanation: The municipality must take up the challenge to actively promote greenery and sustainability and lead the way in this. A green and healthy living environment is something we can benefit from and must stimulate. Preferably by encouraging, connecting, and inspiring. In specific cases, with additional regulations.

230493.explanation: Catering hours are primarily determined by customer demand. We do not support later closing times for pubs deep into the night. The English model, where opening hours start earlier and closing happens before midnight, better fits our Salland tradition.

230499.explanation: The municipality (through the sports department) provides general facilities such as sports halls, gymnasiums, and swimming pools and supports associations in their operations. If associations want to arrange additional own facilities, they are responsible themselves.

230500.explanation: Through local art, culture, and greenery, the municipality contributes to beautifying public spaces. Roundabouts are well suited for enhancing the surroundings for residents and also serve as a showcase for residents and tourists. The design must not compromise traffic safety.

230501.explanation: Yes, via the volunteer support center, associations can be helped to find volunteers and by providing a coordinator who can relieve boards and volunteers of associations.

230503.explanation: Additional enforcement officers (boa's) are desirable in the fight against drug use, public nuisance, and undermining of society.

230504.explanation: There is sufficient evidence that pesticides affect citizens’ health and can lead to brain disorders. Larger pesticide-free zones are justified for the health of residents.

230505.explanation: All residents of Raalte (women, elderly, etc.) must feel safe on the streets. Social safety should be a primary consideration in the design of public spaces. Camera surveillance can contribute to safer public spaces.

230506.explanation: Preventing drug use and addiction is important because it is beneficial for the (mental) health of youth, reduces nuisance, and limits safety risks. More and better enforcement helps prevent young people from easily becoming victims of dealers.

230507.explanation: The municipality must play a central coordinating role to (preventively) combat social undermining. Given the complexity and cross-municipal nature of this issue, cooperation with other municipalities is desirable to have the right expertise.

230510.explanation: The national Health Council advises a distance of 500 meters around goat farms. The municipality must implement this in the licensing rules for goat farms.

230511.explanation: In the policy for the countryside, the municipality should promote conservation and restoration of natural values and biodiversity. This can take the form of 'Farmers with nature' or other innovative income models or living arrangements in the countryside.

230512.explanation: A modern democracy includes close involvement of citizens. This can be achieved by establishing citizen panels, periodic consultations with residents and interest groups on housing and other areas.

230514.explanation: The municipality should support first-time home buyers and enable creative solutions, without stepping into the role of private entrepreneurs.

230515.explanation: Raalte currently aims for 30%, but not as a rigid norm. The development of new homes should be aligned with the expected need in the coming years. Priority for the near future is affordable owner-occupied and rental homes for youth, single-person homes, and flexible housing. Thirty-five percent is too much.

230517.explanation: Culture also includes local village festivals, musical activities of choirs and music groups, and local theaters. These are important for a good living environment and therefore deserve support.

230521.explanation: The principle is that people work meaningfully. Other government organizations are available for job application guidance and finding work.

230523.explanation: Manage parking opportunities to reduce parking pressure. Specifically address unsafe traffic situations.

230524.explanation: Public spaces and public buildings must be easily accessible and suitable for people with disabilities. We want the municipality to achieve this by regularly consulting representatives of these target groups.

230525.explanation: If there are local problems where the parking standard is not met, the municipality must look for an appropriate solution while maintaining traffic safety and combining it with additional charging stations.

230526.explanation: Roads and associated facilities suffer greatly from construction traffic, etc. The current sequence-with temporary emergency pavement and final layout after housing construction-is better.

230527.explanation: Priority for the next administrative period should be at the Bos intersection and the ring road around Mariënheem.

230531.explanation: Volunteers are very important in the social life of Raalte. The municipality supports volunteers in various ways. Informal caregivers are a special group of volunteers and play an important role in providing care. They deserve recognition and encouragement.

230532.explanation: D66 opposes a general obligation. The municipality has a task to maintain an up-to-date overview of institutions. It seeks the most suitable way to inform the different target groups. A printed guide in limited quantity can be part of this.

230473.explanation: With a consultative referendum, residents get a clear voice on important municipal plans. This enhances involvement and trust in the local government.

230475.explanation: If research shows that this is less sustainable and most costly for the future, we are against it.

230476.explanation: Provided thorough research shows this is a future-proof choice, we support it.

230478.explanation: We find it important that expectations about involving residents are clear in advance, so that ultimately no disappointments arise.

230480.explanation: To maintain livability, additional rules are sometimes necessary. These can, for example, be laid down in the General Local Ordinance (APV). This ensures that residents’ enjoyment of living is not limited and that entrepreneurs know where they stand.

230481.explanation: In times of adversity, extra investment is needed to ensure that those who already find it difficult are not further impacted.

230482.explanation: By jointly investing in tourist facilities, we make our municipality even more attractive to visitors.

230483.explanation: Currently, our tourist tax is below the national average. We are in favor of increasing it slightly, provided this does not become a barrier to visiting our municipality.

230484.explanation: Status holders cannot build up registration time. If they do not receive urgency status, this reduces their chances of obtaining a rental home, slowing the flow out of shelters.

230485.explanation: A good distribution is fair and creates more support.

230486.explanation: Learning the Dutch language increases the chance of full participation in society.

230487.explanation: The municipality has a legal obligation to carry this out properly. We want to comply with this.

230488.explanation: The growth of large livestock farms conflicts with livability, health, and climate goals and can lead to displacement of smaller businesses. By setting limits here, the countryside remains future-proof and balanced.

230489.explanation: The province currently prohibits large-scale solar panels on agricultural land. We prefer solar panels, in accordance with national and provincial regulations, on roofs and combined with other functions.

230490.explanation: Well, provided that the livability of residents nearby is taken into good account and is not negatively affected.

230491.explanation: Many pesticides are used in the cultivation of lilies. This is harmful to the health of nearby residents.

230492.explanation: The municipality cannot impose stricter sustainability rules than those allowed nationally. However, we can and want to steer through location choices, land policy, subsidies, and agreements with developers.

230493.explanation: Currently, opening hours for the hospitality industry are already allowed until 2 a.m. We find this generous enough to maintain livability for nearby residents.

230499.explanation: Sports clubs are important for exercise and healthy aging. But sports clubs without their own facilities also deserve support.

230500.explanation: Art and culture connect people, give identity, and thereby strengthen society. When decorating roundabouts with art and culture, traffic safety must be paramount.

230501.explanation: Many associations operate on volunteers who are dedicated with heart and soul. At the same time, regulations increase, putting associations under pressure. Therefore, we find it important that associations are well supported.

230503.explanation: The number of enforcement officers (boa’s) must correspond to the tasks and responsibilities they have in Raalte.

230504.explanation: For the health of our residents, we find it important that they live far from such sites. We want to examine via the Environmental Act (Omgevingswet, BAL) what possibilities exist to legally secure this.

230505.explanation: In places with persistent nuisance, camera surveillance can help reduce nuisance and increase residents' sense of safety.

230506.explanation: Prevention and enforcement reinforce each other. By combining education and guidance with enforcement, drug use among youth can be prevented and discouraged.

230507.explanation: Integrated collaboration in tackling undermining crime is important.

230510.explanation: We find it important that the municipality maintains sufficient distance between goat farms and homes to limit health risks for residents.

230511.explanation: This contributes to biodiversity, water retention, and makes the landscape more attractive and diverse.

230512.explanation: If this council is properly composed and the powers are properly arranged.

230514.explanation: By keeping land owned by the municipality, starters can buy a home with lower monthly costs. This makes it easier for starters to buy their first home.

230515.explanation: The municipality must focus more on affordable rental housing for people with lower incomes.

230517.explanation: Culture connects people, gives identity, and thereby strengthens society.

230521.explanation: We want to encourage people to get the best possible opportunities to re-enter the workforce.

230523.explanation: Fewer cars in the center benefit livability and traffic safety and strengthen the village character. However, good facilities must be available to park cars within a short distance of the center.

230524.explanation: This is already a prerequisite when constructing or renewing bicycle paths.

230525.explanation: By focusing on sustainable mobility, we reduce car use. If parking pressure is still too high and extra parking spaces are needed, we will place them on the edge of a district or neighborhood.

230526.explanation: We start with the main infrastructure and lay roads and public spaces in phases, so construction traffic does not damage permanent streets and the neighborhood becomes safe and future-proof.

230527.explanation: An eastern bypass relieves the N348 and N35, improving traffic flow at the Bos intersection.

230531.explanation: Caregivers are indispensable in our society. They do important work that is not always visible. With a token of appreciation, they receive the recognition and appreciation they deserve.

230532.explanation: Paper municipal guides are expensive and quickly become outdated. Therefore, we focus on well-accessible and up-to-date online information about care and welfare services.

statements.title: The opening hours of restaurants and cafés should be extended.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality determines the opening hours of restaurants and cafes. These rules are in the General Local Regulation (APV). Currently restaurants and cafes in the municipality of Raalte must be closed to visitors between 02:00 and 07:00.

statements.title: Residents must be given the possibility to organize a consultative referendum to express their opinion on a municipal plan.

titleParts.text: Inwoners moeten de mogelijkheid krijgen een raadgevend

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

titleParts.text: te organiseren. Zij kunnen dan hun mening geven over een plan van de gemeente.

statements.moreInfo: In an advisory referendum residents can give their opinion on a municipal plan or decision by voting for or against a single issue. The result is an advice to the municipal council, which means the council ultimately decides and is not obliged to follow the outcome.

statements.title: The tourist tax must be increased. The rate is now €1.55 per overnight stay per person.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

titleParts.text: moet omhoog. Het tarief is nu € 1,55 per overnachting per persoon.

statements.title: The municipality must not impose extra rules on entrepreneurs beyond national regulations.
The municipality must speed up the legally required reception of asylum seekers.
Raalte must thoroughly renovate the existing town hall.

titleParts.text: Raalte moet het bestaande gemeentehuis grondig

titleParts.glossary: Repairing or partially renewing an existing building.

statements.title: Recognized refugees should not be given priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.glossary: Refugees who have been granted a residence permit and are therefore allowed to stay in the Netherlands.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must take measures against the growth of large livestock farms in the rural area.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet maatregelen nemen tegen de groei van grote

titleParts.glossary: Farms where animals are kept, such as cows or pigs.
Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.title: Raalte must spread the reception of asylum seekers across multiple locations in the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

titleParts.text: spreiden over meerdere plekken in de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality must tighten the rules for lily cultivation in the rural area.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de regels voor de teelt van lelies in het

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.title: The municipality must spend more money on Dutch language lessons for newcomers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan Nederlandse taallessen voor

titleParts.glossary: People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality must not introduce stricter sustainability rules than national regulations.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geen strengere regels voor

titleParts.glossary: Sustainability is taking into account the consequences for people, the environment and the future.

statements.title: The municipality must encourage converting agricultural land in Raalte into nature reserves.
The buffer zones around homes where spraying of pesticides is prohibited must be wider than the national requirement.

titleParts.text: De zones rond woningen waarbinnen het spuiten van

titleParts.glossary: Substances used to control weeds, insects or diseases.

titleParts.text: verboden is moeten ruimer worden dan wat landelijk verplicht is.

statements.title: It must remain possible for first-time buyers to buy a home while the land remains owned by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: People buying a house for the first time who have never had a mortgage.

titleParts.text: om een woning te kopen terwijl de grond eigendom blijft van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality must give extra money to sports clubs that have their own facilities.
The municipality must establish a Council for Housing Applicants that participates in housing policy discussions.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet een Raad voor Woningzoekenden instellen die meepraat over het woon

titleParts.glossary: A plan for how a problem will be addressed.

statements.title: The municipality may only permit housing construction once the required roads and other facilities have been built.

titleParts.text: De gemeente mag woningbouw pas toestaan als de benodigde wegen en andere

titleParts.glossary: Buildings or services needed for daily life, such as roads, sports facilities or healthcare.

statements.title: Raalte must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs).

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

statements.moreInfo: Enforcement officers (BOAs) provide supervision and enforcement in Raalte’s public space. They address people causing nuisance, check that rules are followed, and work with the police. Raalte currently has 2 BOAs working a total of 1.6 FTE. FTE means full-time equivalent: 1.6 FTE equals the hours of 1.6 full-time jobs. Because BOAs always operate in pairs, practical street deployment is 0.8 FTE.

statements.title: Of the newly built homes in Raalte, at least 35% must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Raalte moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: Social rental homes are low-rent houses intended for people with lower incomes. In 2026 the maximum rent at the start of a social rental contract may be €932.93. About 24% of the current homes in Raalte are social rental. For new construction the municipality agrees on the share of social rental homes. Often the 30-40-30 split is used: 30% social rental, 40% affordable owner-occupied, 30% higher-priced owner-occupied. This is also the rule in Raalte. The proposal concerns raising the share of social rental to at least 35%.

statements.title: The municipality must give informal caregivers an annual token of appreciation.

titleParts.glossary: Someone who provides unpaid and often long-term care for sick family members or friends.
A small gift or gesture to show appreciation.

statements.title: The municipality of Raalte must install CCTV in locations with nuisance problems.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Cars must be kept out of the centers of the villages.
The municipality must spend less money on culture.
The municipality of Raalte must annually distribute a printed municipal guide with an overview of care and welfare services.

titleParts.text: De gemeente Raalte moet jaarlijks een papieren gemeentegids verspreiden met een overzicht van zorg- en

titleParts.glossary: Activities and services that help people take part in society.

statements.title: The municipality must provide practical support to jobseekers, for example with transport and application guidance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet werkzoekenden praktisch ondersteunen, bijvoorbeeld met vervoer en

titleParts.glossary: Help with searching for work and applying for jobs.

statements.title: More parking spaces must be created in residential neighborhoods in the municipality of Raalte.
The municipality must advocate for an eastern bypass around Raalte with a connection to the N35.
The municipality must design roundabouts with art and cultural features.

shootoutStatements.title: Raalte must build a new town hall in the Spoorzone.
In case of budget shortfalls, the municipality must reduce spending and not raise taxes.
Raalte must invest more in involving citizens in municipal policymaking.
The municipality must, together with local businesses, invest more in tourist amenities such as benches and signs.
The municipality must allow solar panels on vacant land, including next to homes.
The municipality must step up enforcement against drug use among young people.
The municipality must invest money in supporting associations with administrative tasks.
Raalte must, together with neighboring municipalities, appoint a coordinator to tackle undermining criminality. Criminals make use of government, businesses and services for their illegal activities.

shootoutStatements.theme: Bike paths for people with disabilities
shootoutStatements.title: When building or renovating bike paths, accessibility for people with disabilities must be a strict requirement.
The municipality must ensure sufficient distance between goat farms and homes to reduce health risks.
